headlines.headline: Ukraine Strikes Three Major Russian Energy Facilities Overnight
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces targeted key Russian energy infrastructure in overnight operations, continuing their campaign against Russian oil refining capabilities as the war enters a new phase.

headlines.headline: Europe Fears Putin May Exploit US Distraction in Middle East
headlines.summary: NATO officials express concern that Russia sees a window of opportunity while US attention is focused on the Iran conflict, potentially testing alliance resolve in Eastern Europe.

headlines.headline: Trump-Xi Summit Weighs Iran Oil, Taiwan and US Exports
headlines.summary: The high-stakes meeting between Trump and Xi in Beijing will cover the Iran conflict impact on global oil markets, Taiwan tensions, and trade relations between the superpowers.

headlines.headline: Belgian Defense Minister Praises Turkish Defense Industry
headlines.summary: Belgium defense chief calls Turkiye a model to follow in defense production, highlighting the growing prominence of Turkish defense exports and technology in European security discussions.

headlines.headline: OPEC Slashes 2026 Global Oil Demand Growth Forecast
headlines.summary: The cartel lowered its demand growth projections as the Iran war creates uncertainty in energy markets, signaling potential production adjustments ahead.

headlines.headline: India Seeks Russian Oil Waiver Extension from US
headlines.summary: India has asked the US to extend its waiver on Russian oil imports as the Iran war disrupts Persian Gulf energy supplies, Bloomberg News reported.

headlines.headline: India Considers Tax Cuts on Foreign Bond Investments
headlines.summary: India is considering significant reductions in taxes paid by foreign investors on bonds as authorities seek to align policies with global norms and attract inflows.

headlines.headline: Xi Hails New Positioning in US-China Relations
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping praised a "new positioning" of ties with the US that envisages cooperation with measured competition following his summit with Trump.

headlines.headline: Cuba runs out of diesel and fuel oil as US blockade squeezes supply
headlines.summary: Cuba Energy Minister Vicente de la O Levy announced the country has completely run out of diesel and fuel oil, with Havana facing 20-22 hour blackouts. The US has offered m in aid in exchange for reforms to the communist system.

headlines.headline: Republican Senator Murkowski breaks with Trump on Iran war
headlines.summary: Senator Lisa Murkowski voted against the Iran war for the first time, joining Republicans Susan Collins and Rand Paul in a 49-50 vote. GOP unease grows as high gas prices and constitutional concerns mount ahead of November elections.

headlines.headline: South Korea Cheongung-II missile system confirmed in combat use against Iranian missiles
headlines.summary: South Korean military officials confirmed the Cheongung-II surface-to-air missile system successfully intercepted Iranian ballistic missiles during the Middle East conflict, marking the first confirmed combat shootdown by a Korean weapons system.

perspectives.angle: South Korea showcases Cheongung-II missile defense and KF-21 fighter in military demonstration
perspectives.summary: South Korea's military offered a rare look at its flagship defense programs, demonstrating the Cheongung-II missile defense system and KF-21 fighter aircraft amid regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Russia establishes full partnership with Afghan Taliban, urges regional cooperation
headlines.summary: Russia is building a full-fledged partnership with Afghanistans Taliban government, encouraging SCO members to expand cooperation with Kabul in security, trade, and humanitarian support.

headlines.headline: US Army general: Industry surge and allied burden sharing essential for Indo-Pacific peace
headlines.summary: Gen. Xavier Brunson emphasized that military strength, industrial sustainability, and allied collaboration are critical components to create a fortress chain of defense burden sharing in the Indo-Pacific region.

headlines.headline: Samsung union threatens 18-day strike amid AI chip boom, sparking economic slowdown fears
headlines.summary: Samsung Electronics largest union has threatened an 18-day walkout from May 21 to June 7, raising concerns about production disruptions and wider fallout across the global technology supply chain.

headlines.headline: Israeli strikes kill 12 in Lebanon ahead of US-mediated ceasefire talks
headlines.summary: Israeli airstrikes hit cars on a highway south of Beirut, killing 12 people, as Lebanon and Israel prepare for a third round of Washington-mediated talks with their current ceasefire set to expire within days.

perspectives.angle: Israeli strikes kill 8 including 2 children on Lebanese highway despite ceasefire
perspectives.summary: At least eight people, including two children, were killed when Israeli airstrikes targeted cars on a coastal highway south of Beirut, the latest violation of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire that has now seen 380 deaths.

headlines.headline: Russia unleashes largest aerial assault on Ukraine, pummeling Kyiv with hundreds of drones
headlines.summary: Russia launched the biggest aerial attack on Ukraine over a two-day period since the war began, striking Kyiv and multiple cities with hundreds of drones in a devastating barrage that killed at least two and wounded dozens.

headlines.headline: Iran war devastates Middle East economies and upends regional security
headlines.summary: The US-Israeli bombing campaign against Iran has damaged infrastructure across the region, exposed Gulf energy vulnerabilities, and overturned decades of assumptions about Middle East security architecture.

perspectives.angle: How the Iran War Has Reshaped Middle East Alliances and Economies
perspectives.summary: A comprehensive analysis of how the US-Israeli bombing campaign that began February 28 has destabilized the region, damaged infrastructure and economies, and overturned assumptions about regional security.

perspectives.angle: Saudi Arabia Launched Covert Attacks on Iran During Regional War
perspectives.summary: Saudi Arabia carried out previously unreported military strikes on Iranian soil and targeted Tehran-backed militias in Iraq, marking the first known direct Saudi military action against Iran.

headlines.headline: NATO eastern flank leaders call for consolidated air defenses after Russian violations
headlines.summary: Leaders of 14 NATO allies on the eastern flank warned that repeated Russian airspace breaches underscore the urgent need to strengthen the alliance's air defense capabilities against missiles and drones.

perspectives.angle: NATO leaders call for stronger eastern flank air defense after Russian breaches
perspectives.summary: Leaders of 14 NATO allies said Russia repeated airspace violations underline the urgent need to consolidate alliance air defenses against missiles and drones.

headlines.headline: Xi Warns Trump That Taiwan Could Push US-China Relations Into "Great Jeopardy"
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping issued a stark warning to President Trump during their Beijing summit, saying US-China relations could enter an "extremely dangerous place" if the Taiwan issue is not handled properly. The warning came as the leaders discussed trade and the Iran conflict.

perspectives.angle: Xi Warns Trump of US-China Clash if Taiwan Issue Mishandled
perspectives.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping told Trump during their Beijing summit that Taiwan must be handled properly or the two nations will face conflict. Xi called for the US and China to be partners rather than rivals as the high-stakes meeting began.

headlines.headline: Iran Says Ready to Repel New US Attack as Peace Talks Stall
headlines.summary: Iran's military said it is trained and ready for any new US assault as President Trump predicted a "long talk" about the war during his China visit. Peace talks remain stalled with no breakthrough in sight.

headlines.headline: Philippines seeks ICC suspect after Senate shootout chaos
headlines.summary: Philippine authorities are hunting for Senator Ronald "Bato" dela Rosa wanted by the ICC after gunfire erupted at the Senate building where he had taken refuge. Marcos convened an emergency meeting.

headlines.headline: Russia places UK ex-defense minister Ben Wallace on wanted list
headlines.summary: Russia has placed former British Defence Minister Ben Wallace on its wanted list in connection with an unspecified criminal investigation, state media reported Wednesday citing the Interior Ministry.

headlines.headline: Heavy Russian strikes kill one, wound 31 in Kyiv
headlines.summary: Russia pummeled Kyiv and other regions in a massive aerial attack that killed one person and wounded at least 31 others, according to city authorities on Thursday.

perspectives.angle: Russian Strikes Kill 8 in Dnipropetrovsk Region Overnight
perspectives.summary: Russian overnight strikes on Ukraine's Dnipropetrovsk region killed eight people and injured at least 11 as Moscow continued its aerial campaign.

headlines.headline: M23 Rebels and Rwandan Soldiers Accused of War Crimes in DR Congo
headlines.summary: Human Rights Watch documented summary executions, rape, and forced recruitment during M23's occupation of Uvira in eastern DR Congo. At least 53 civilians were executed and eight cases of rape were documented. Rwanda denies supporting M23 despite UN evidence of its control over rebel operations.

headlines.headline: Quarter of World Cup Games at Risk of Dangerous Heat, Scientists Warn
headlines.summary: Climate change has increased the risk of extreme heat at the 2026 World Cup, with 26 of 104 matches potentially facing conditions that require cooling breaks. The final has a 1-in-8 chance of reaching heat levels that could force postponement.

headlines.headline: Iran war overshadows Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
headlines.summary: Donald Trump is expected to ask China to help end the costly and unpopular Iran war in discussions with Xi Jinping, with peace talks stalled and the global economic cost of the conflict increasing.

perspectives.angle: Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing: Iran War and Trade Top Agenda
perspectives.summary: President Trump meets Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing for high-stakes talks on the Iran conflict and trade relations. Trump says he does not need China's help on Iran, but analysts say Beijing's cooperation is critical for any diplomatic resolution.

headlines.headline: Gold rises as markets watch Trump-Xi talks and Iran war
headlines.summary: Gold prices ticked up on Thursday, supported by a weaker dollar, as investors focused on talks between Trump and Xi Jinping and looked for signs of progress in the Iran war.

perspectives.angle: Indian rupee hits all-time low as Iran war shadows Trump China trip
perspectives.summary: The Indian rupee is expected to remain under pressure, holding near its record low, as persistent oil-linked headwinds from the Iran war continue to weigh on the currency during Trump's high-stakes summit with Xi.

headlines.headline: Oil markets look to Xi-Trump summit for Iran war relief
headlines.summary: The increasingly stressed crude oil market is looking to the Xi-Trump summit for any signs of progress that could help de-escalate the Iran war and ease supply concerns.

perspectives.angle: IEA Warns Global Oil Supply Will Fall Below Demand Due to Iran War
perspectives.summary: The International Energy Agency revised its outlook, warning that global oil supply will fall short of demand this year as the Iran war disrupts Middle East production and drains inventories at an unprecedented pace.

headlines.headline: Chinese yuan hits 3-year high ahead of Trump-Xi summit
headlines.summary: China's yuan edged up to a three-year high against the dollar, while stock indexes dipped, as investors awaited the outcome of the summit between Trump and Xi.

headlines.headline: US House members force vote on Ukraine aid, defying leadership
headlines.summary: A U.S. House petition to force a floor vote on providing security aid to Ukraine and imposing new sanctions on Russia reached the 218-signature threshold, defying Republican leadership.

perspectives.angle: House Lawmakers Reach Threshold to Force Vote on Ukraine Aid Package
perspectives.summary: A discharge petition to force a House vote on Ukraine security aid and new Russia sanctions reached 218 signatures, the latest successful bid by lawmakers to defy Republican leadership.

headlines.headline: Iran War Casts Shadow Over BRICS Foreign Ministers Meeting in Delhi
headlines.summary: The US-Israeli war on Iran is testing BRICS unity as foreign ministers gather in New Delhi, with the bloc struggling to reach a unified position on the conflict that has disrupted global oil markets.

perspectives.angle: BRICS Foreign Ministers Meet in Delhi as Iran War Tests Bloc Unity
perspectives.summary: Foreign ministers from BRICS nations began a two-day meeting in New Delhi, with the Iran-US conflict, oil prices, and internal divisions dominating the agenda. India hosts the summit amid efforts to maintain bloc cohesion on West Asian crises.

headlines.headline: Trump-Xi Summit Begins in Beijing with Trade and Iran War on Agenda
headlines.summary: President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping opened high-stakes talks in Beijing, with Xi saying the two nations should be "partners not rivals." Trump brought CEOs including Nvidia's Jensen Huang and Elon Musk, seeking China's help on the Iran war and trade cooperation.

perspectives.angle: Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing Ends After Two Hours of Talks
perspectives.summary: The high-stakes meeting between President Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping concluded after 2 hours and 15 minutes. The summit addressed trade disputes, Taiwan tensions, and the Iran war. This marked the first visit by a U.S. president to America's main strategic rival since 2017.

headlines.headline: Russia Oil Output Drops 460,000 Barrels Per Day Amid Ukraine Drone Attacks
headlines.summary: Russian crude production fell to 8.8 million barrels per day in April, down 460,000 bpd year-on-year, as Ukraine intensified drone strikes on energy infrastructure, according to the IEA.

headlines.headline: Warsh Confirmed as Fed Chair Amid Rising Inflation Pressures
headlines.summary: The US Senate confirmed Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair by a 54-45 vote, the most partisan confirmation ever for a Fed chair, as inflation concerns mount from oil price shocks linked to the Iran war.

headlines.headline: China stages naval drills near Philippines as US launches Balikatan 2026
headlines.summary: China's PLA Southern Theatre Command conducted naval exercises east of Luzon, coinciding with the launch of US-Philippines Balikatan 2026 drills featuring unprecedented Japanese participation.

headlines.headline: Germany opens trial of "Ulm 5" activists over Elbit arms firm attack
headlines.summary: Five activists face trial in Stuttgart for a September 2025 attack on an Israeli arms manufacturer's German subsidiary, charged under controversial criminal organization statutes.

perspectives.angle: UK Activists Face Terrorist Sentencing for Raid on Israeli Arms Firm
perspectives.summary: Four Palestine Action members convicted of smashing a UK site of Israeli defense firm Elbit could be sentenced as terrorists, marking a significant escalation in legal consequences for pro-Palestine activism.

headlines.headline: Chinese AI satire of US-Iran war goes viral on state media
headlines.summary: A wuxia-style animated allegory by CCTV depicting the "White Eagle" vs "Persian Cat" conflict has triggered user-made spin-offs, reflecting Chinese public engagement with the Iran war narrative.

headlines.headline: Iran explores yuan-for-Hormuz oil trade plan, Chinese analysts urge caution
headlines.summary: Reports suggest Iran may allow oil tankers through the Strait of Hormuz if trade is conducted in Chinese yuan, but analysts warn of implementation challenges and risks to China-US relations.

headlines.headline: Senate confirms Kevin Warsh as new Fed Chair
headlines.summary: The US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve Chair in a 54-45 vote, the most partisan confirmation ever for a Fed chair. Warsh replaces Jerome Powell as inflation concerns mount amid the Iran war oil shock.

perspectives.angle: Kevin Warsh Set for Senate Confirmation as Fed Chair Amid Inflation Surge
perspectives.summary: The US Senate is expected to confirm Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair as inflation hits a three-year high, with markets watching for potential rate hikes.

headlines.headline: Ukraine strikes Russian oil terminal, refinery and gas plant
headlines.summary: Ukraine resumed attacks on Russian energy infrastructure after a short-lived ceasefire, hitting an oil terminal, refinery and gas processing plant in response to Russia's massive drone assault that killed six civilians in western Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine Resumes Strikes on Russian Energy Infrastructure After Ceasefire Ends
perspectives.summary: Ukraine renewed attacks on Russian oil refineries and ports after a US-brokered ceasefire expired. The escalation comes as peace talks stall and both sides intensify energy infrastructure targeting.

headlines.headline: Switzerland eyes rival air defenses after US delays Patriot delivery
headlines.summary: Switzerland said it will explore air-defense systems from other suppliers after the US informed it that Patriot missile deliveries will be further delayed due to the Iran war, highlighting how the conflict is straining global weapons supplies.

headlines.headline: Zambia cuts rates despite Iran war inflation risks
headlines.summary: Zambia's central bank lowered its main interest rate, saying it sees inflation staying within target range despite risks from the Iran war, becoming one of the few central banks to ease amid global inflationary pressures.

headlines.headline: US House unanimously passes resolution urging Trump to press Xi on China detainees
headlines.summary: The House voted unanimously to urge President Trump to raise the issue of American detainees in China during his meeting with President Xi.

headlines.headline: ECB Warns Iran Oil Shock May Force Interest Rate Hikes
headlines.summary: European Central Bank chief economist Philip Lane warned that a sustained oil shock from the Iran war may require interest rate increases to prevent higher fuel costs from spreading to wages and broader prices.

perspectives.angle: ECB Warns of Rate Hikes if Iran War Oil Shock Deanchors Inflation
perspectives.summary: European Central Bank chief economist Philip Lane warned that a global oil shock from the Iran war may require raising interest rates to prevent fuel costs from spreading to wages and broader prices. The warning highlights mounting economic pressure from the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US producer prices surge most in four years as Iran war drives inflation
perspectives.summary: US producer prices posted their biggest increase in four years in April, boosted by soaring costs for goods and services, the latest sign of accelerating inflation amid the war with Iran.

perspectives.angle: Fed officials signal rate hikes may be needed to curb inflation
perspectives.summary: Boston Fed President Collins and Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari both indicated interest rates may need to rise if inflation pressures persist, with the Iran war worsening price pressures.

headlines.headline: Kremlin Says Russia Ready for US Business Ties If Ukraine Link Dropped
headlines.summary: Russia expressed interest in joint economic projects with the United States if Washington stops linking trade relations to a Ukraine peace deal, signaling potential flexibility in US-Russia relations.

headlines.headline: Iran releases prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on bail
headlines.summary: Iran freed prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh, who was arrested in April during a crackdown amid the conflict with the US and Israel. Her release on bail marks a rare concession during wartime.

perspectives.angle: Iran releases prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on bail
perspectives.summary: Iran has freed prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on bail after her arrest in early April during a crackdown. Sotoudeh is a leading Iranian rights defender who has been imprisoned multiple times for her activism.

headlines.headline: Russia places former UK Defence Minister Ben Wallace on wanted list
headlines.summary: Russia has placed former British defence minister Ben Wallace on its wanted list in connection with an unspecified criminal investigation, escalating tensions with the UK.

perspectives.angle: Russia Places Former UK Defense Minister Ben Wallace on Wanted List
perspectives.summary: Moscow added Ben Wallace to its wanted list in connection with an unspecified criminal investigation, escalating tensions with Britain over Ukraine support.

headlines.headline: Turkey and Armenia move closer to direct trade links
headlines.summary: Turkey lifted some customs restrictions on Armenia, paving the way for direct trade between the two countries in a further sign of normalizing ties after more than 30 years of strained relations.

headlines.headline: Hungary summons Russian ambassador over drone strikes on western Ukraine
headlines.summary: Hungary summoned Russia's ambassador after unprecedented drone attacks hit western Ukraine near its border. The strikes came hours after PM Orban criticized EU support for Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Russian Forces Ordered to Behead Ukrainian Soldiers, Ukraine General Staff Reports
headlines.summary: Ukraines General Staff reports that Russian forces were ordered to behead and display the bodies of two fallen Ukrainian soldiers, calling it a deliberate violation of the rules of war.

headlines.headline: Hungary Summons Russian Ambassador After Zakarpattia Drone Attack
headlines.summary: Hungary summoned Russias ambassador after Ukraines western Zakarpattia Oblast, home to a Hungarian minority, was targeted in Russias mass drone attack that killed at least six people.

headlines.headline: US Army Abruptly Cancels Poland Deployment, Europe Personnel at Pre-2022 Levels
headlines.summary: The US Army has abruptly halted a planned deployment of 4,000 soldiers to Poland, bringing American personnel numbers in Europe to pre-2022 levels before Russias invasion of Ukraine.

headlines.headline: Air Force MQ-9 Reaper Fleet Drops to 135 Aircraft After Iran Combat Losses
headlines.summary: The US Air Force is seeking to backfill its MQ-9 Reaper inventory after combat losses in the Iran conflict, while fielding a cheaper, more expendable replacement drone.

headlines.headline: Austrian Jets Intercept Unauthorized US Military Aircraft Two Days in a Row
headlines.summary: Austrian fighter jets intercepted US military aircraft flying through Austrian airspace without authorization on consecutive days, highlighting tensions over military flight corridors.

headlines.headline: Russia Launches Massive Drone Barrage on Western Ukraine, Killing Six
headlines.summary: Russia fired approximately 800 drones at about 20 Ukrainian regions in a daylight attack targeting critical infrastructure near NATO borders, killing at least six people and prompting Poland to scramble fighter jets.

perspectives.angle: Russia Launches Massive Drone and Missile Barrage on Ukraine, Killing One
perspectives.summary: Moscow fired more than 700 drones and missiles at Ukraine in a deadly night of attacks that killed at least one person in Kyiv and wounded dozens. The assault came after a brief ceasefire ended.

headlines.headline: US Senate Blocks Bid to Restrain Trump Iran War Powers as Support Grows
headlines.summary: Senate Republicans blocked the latest Democratic effort to end the Iran war without congressional authorization, but a third Republican voted to advance the bill, signaling growing bipartisan concern over the conflict.

perspectives.angle: US Senate blocks Iran war powers challenge as conflict continues
perspectives.summary: Senate Republicans blocked a Democratic-led effort to end the Iran war until Congress authorizes it, while support for congressional oversight grows.

perspectives.angle: Trump Says Iran Ceasefire on "Life Support" After Rejecting Tehran Peace Proposal
perspectives.summary: President Trump dismissed Iran's counter-offer to end the war as "garbage," saying the ceasefire was on "massive life support." Iran demanded sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz and war reparations while rejecting any removal of its enriched uranium.

perspectives.angle: Trump proposes suspending federal gasoline tax amid Iran war
perspectives.summary: President Trump announced plans to suspend the federal gasoline tax to ease prices as the Iran war continues to disrupt oil markets and keep the Strait of Hormuz closed. Critics note he cannot implement this unilaterally without Congress.

headlines.headline: EU warns of risks from growing reliance on US gas
headlines.summary: European energy regulators warned that the EU increasing dependence on U.S. gas carries risks, including rising reliance on a single supplier.

headlines.headline: US Navy Could Run Out of Money by July, Top Officer Warns
headlines.summary: Chief of Naval Operations Adm. Daryl Caudle told lawmakers the Navy might have to modify operations if Congress does not pass a budget, highlighting funding concerns amid ongoing global deployments.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Coalition Submits Bill for Early Elections in Israel
headlines.summary: Israel's ruling coalition moved to dissolve parliament, potentially triggering snap elections amid domestic political pressures during the ongoing Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: US Renews $100M Cuba Aid Offer as Trump Raises Island with Xi
headlines.summary: Washington offered renewed humanitarian assistance to Cuba while Trump discussed the island nation in talks with Chinese leadership, highlighting competing influence efforts in the Caribbean.

headlines.headline: Iran War Driving Global Shift Toward Chinese Clean Energy
headlines.summary: The conflict with Iran is accelerating countries' transition to Chinese EVs, solar panels and batteries, strengthening Beijing's position ahead of Trump-Xi trade negotiations.

headlines.headline: Iran War Brings Turkey-PKK Peace Process to Near Standstill
headlines.summary: The regional turmoil from the Iran conflict has derailed Turkey's peace process with Kurdish militants. Just weeks after parliament advanced reconciliation efforts, the war plunged the region into new instability.

headlines.headline: Latvia Government Loses Majority Over Ukraine Drone Incident Response
headlines.summary: Latvia's ruling coalition lost its parliamentary majority after the Progressives party withdrew support over the government's handling of Ukraine-related drone incidents, deepening European political instability.

headlines.headline: Gunshots at Philippine Senate as ICC-Wanted Senator Resists Arrest
headlines.summary: Shots were fired inside the Philippine Senate as troops moved to arrest Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity during Duterte's drug war.

perspectives.angle: Gunshots Erupt at Philippine Senate During ICC Arrest Standoff
perspectives.summary: Gunshots broke out at the Philippine Senate as security forces attempted to arrest Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity related to the drug war.

headlines.headline: Nigeria Urges Global Finance Overhaul as Debt Costs Consume Budget
headlines.summary: President Tinubu warned that Nigeria will spend $11.6 billion on debt servicing in 2026 - nearly half of government revenue - calling for reform of a financial system that penalizes African borrowers.

headlines.headline: Nigeria urges global finance overhaul as debt costs consume half of revenue
headlines.summary: President Tinubu called for reforming the global financial system, saying Nigeria will spend .6 billion on debt service in 2026 - nearly half of projected government revenue.

headlines.headline: Bank of England to hold rates at 3.75% as Iran war fuels inflation
headlines.summary: The BoE will likely keep rates steady this year, though a growing minority of economists expect a hike as energy price surges from the Iran conflict drive up inflation forecasts.

headlines.headline: Netanyahu Held Secret Meeting With UAE President During Iran War
headlines.summary: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu visited the UAE and met with the Emirati president during the Iran war, marking their first confirmed meeting and a "historic breakthrough" in relations.

perspectives.angle: UAE Denies Netanyahu Made Secret Visit During Iran War
perspectives.summary: The United Arab Emirates denied reports that Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu secretly visited the country during the Israel-US war with Iran. The denial comes amid heightened regional diplomacy.

headlines.headline: Gulf states arrest over 100 Shiites on treason charges amid Iran war
headlines.summary: Gulf nations have arrested more than 100 Shiites and accused them of treason as the Iran-Israel war continues, according to a New York Times report. The arrests highlight regional sectarian tensions amplified by the conflict.

headlines.headline: Germany calls for probe into alleged Israeli abuse of Palestinian detainees
headlines.summary: Germany has urged an investigation into allegations of torture and sexual abuse against Palestinian detainees in Israeli custody. The call adds international pressure over treatment of prisoners amid the broader regional conflict.

headlines.headline: Japan fails to secure Trump stop in Tokyo before Beijing summit
headlines.summary: Japanese government plans to invite President Trump to Japan ahead of his China visit fell through. The diplomatic setback comes as Trump arrives in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: Anduril raises $5 billion to build autonomous warship operations
headlines.summary: Defense tech company Anduril has secured $5 billion in financing to build autonomous warship operations in Seattle. The major investment signals growing private sector involvement in defense manufacturing.

headlines.headline: Sweden Announces $1.9 Billion Energy Relief Package Amid Middle East War
headlines.summary: Sweden will temporarily reduce fuel taxes and introduce measures to shield households and businesses from soaring energy costs caused by the Iran war disruption.

headlines.headline: House Reaches Threshold to Force Vote on $1.3 Billion Ukraine Aid
headlines.summary: A discharge petition reached 218 signatures to force a House vote on $1.3 billion in Ukraine aid, marking a significant procedural win for supporters.

headlines.headline: France probes Israeli firm BlackCore over alleged election interference
headlines.summary: French authorities are investigating whether an obscure Israeli company carried out a foreign interference campaign targeting hard-left France Unbowed candidates ahead of March municipal elections, including deceptive websites and disparaging digital ads.

headlines.headline: US and China launch unprecedented joint operation against Dubai scam centres
headlines.summary: The FBI and Chinese Ministry of Public Security collaborated with Dubai Police to dismantle nine fraud centres and arrest 276 suspects, in a rare show of cooperation ahead of Trump's China visit.

perspectives.angle: US and China Cooperate Against Dubai Scam Centres
perspectives.summary: US and China are working together in unprecedented cooperation to target scam centres operating out of Dubai, marking a rare area of collaboration amid tensions.

headlines.headline: Australia joins UK-France Hormuz mission as regional tensions escalate
headlines.summary: Australia announced it will participate in a "strictly defensive" mission led by France and Britain to secure shipping through the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing US-Iran tensions.

headlines.headline: Ethiopia-Eritrea war fears rise as Tigray tensions resurface
headlines.summary: The TPLF's reassertion of control in Tigray after electing Debretsion Gebremichael is raising fears of renewed conflict between Ethiopia's federal government and Eritrea, threatening regional stability in the Horn of Africa.

headlines.headline: US special operations leaders frustrated by inability to modify own equipment
headlines.summary: US special operations commanders told Congress that manufacturers' proprietary agreements prevent them from quickly upgrading military equipment, especially unmanned systems, leaving forces with outdated capabilities as technology evolves rapidly.

headlines.headline: Jason Collins, NBA First Openly Gay Player, Dies at 47
headlines.summary: Jason Collins, who made history in 2013 as the NBA first openly gay player, has died from brain cancer at age 47. Collins spent 13 seasons in the league and became a pioneer for inclusion in professional sports.

headlines.headline: Macron Announces 27 Billion in New African Investments
headlines.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced 23 billion euros in investments in Africa at a summit in Kenya, with 14 billion from French companies and 9 billion from African entities. The Africa Forward Summit marks a shift toward sovereign equality in France-Africa relations.

headlines.headline: EU Gas Reliance on US Raises Supply Risk Concerns
headlines.summary: European energy regulators warned that the EU's growing dependence on U.S. liquefied natural gas carries risks, including rising dependence on a single supplier.

headlines.headline: US LNG Vessels Resume China Shipments After Year-Long Pause Ahead of Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: China may receive its first direct U.S. liquefied natural gas shipment in over a year, signaling thawing energy ties as Trump heads to Beijing for talks with Xi Jinping.

headlines.headline: Post-War Gaza Envoy Visits Jerusalem to Advance Ceasefire Deal
headlines.summary: The top diplomat overseeing the U.S.-brokered Gaza ceasefire deal is in Jerusalem to advance implementation of the agreement.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian forces deploy ground robot to destroy Russian-held building
headlines.summary: Ukraines 100th Mechanized Brigade used a kamikaze ground robot to attack a building occupied by Russian sabotage troops in Kostiantynivka, demonstrating evolving robotic warfare tactics on the battlefield.

headlines.headline: Corruption Charges Hit Zelenskiy's Inner Circle as Ukraine War Drags On
headlines.summary: After years of graft allegations swirling around his associates, President Zelenskiy now faces a bigger test with corruption charges reaching his former right-hand man. The case could weaken Ukraine's wartime leadership at a critical juncture.

perspectives.angle: Ukraine anti-corruption court places Zelenskiy ally Yermak under arrest
perspectives.summary: Former presidential chief of staff Andriy Yermak has been arrested on money-laundering charges in a major corruption probe, marking a dramatic fall for Zelenskiy's once most powerful aide.

headlines.headline: US and China Weigh Tariff Cuts on $30 Billion of Imports
headlines.summary: Washington and Beijing are expected to move toward a managed trade mechanism, with each side potentially identifying $30 billion in goods for tariff reductions. The deal would avoid national security red lines while easing trade tensions.

headlines.headline: Britain to Strengthen EU Ties in New Legislative Agenda
headlines.summary: King Charles delivered the governments legislative agenda including legislation to strengthen ties with the European Union, as Prime Minister Keir Starmer fights to stay in power amid leadership pressure.

headlines.headline: Israel Escalates Gaza Strikes as Hamas Regroups After Iran Truce
headlines.summary: Israel has intensified attacks on Gaza in the five weeks since halting joint bombing with the US in Iran, redirecting fire back to the Palestinian enclave where Hamas fighters are tightening their grip.

perspectives.angle: Israel Strikes Lebanon as Deaths Reach 380 Since Truce
perspectives.summary: Israeli strikes continue in Lebanon as authorities report 380 people killed since the ceasefire agreement, raising concerns about truce violations.

headlines.headline: OECD Projects Bank of Japan to Raise Rates to 2% by End-2027
headlines.summary: The OECD forecasts Japan will raise its policy rate to 2% from 0.75% by end-2027, as robust domestic demand helps the economy absorb external shocks from the Middle East conflict.

headlines.headline: Trumps China Summit Ambitions Shrink from Grand Bargain to Plea
headlines.summary: President Trumps ambitions for his summit with Xi Jinping have shrunk from a grand bargain to a plea for help on Iran, as tech rivalry and distrust sap hopes for substantive AI commitments.

headlines.headline: Vietnam seeks US approval for oil tanker to bypass Hormuz blockade
headlines.summary: Vietnam has asked the US to allow an "extremely important" oil tanker carrying nearly 2 million barrels to pass through the Strait of Hormuz blockade. The ship is destined for Nghi Son refinery, which can process 200,000 barrels per day.

perspectives.angle: Oil Tankers Go Dark to Exit the Strait of Hormuz Amid Blockade
perspectives.summary: Vessels are disabling their AIS transponders to navigate through the contested waterway as the US-Iran conflict continues to disrupt global energy flows and shipping routes.

headlines.headline: UAE designates 21 Lebanese citizens and groups as terrorists over Hezbollah ties
headlines.summary: The United Arab Emirates has branded 21 Lebanese individuals and organizations as terrorists, ordering asset freezes and accusing them of connections to Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Iran Military Ready for New US Assault as Trump Heads to China Summit
headlines.summary: Iran's military says it is trained and prepared to repel any new US attack, with peace talks stalled and Trump predicting a 'long talk' about the war with Xi Jinping in Beijing. The fragile ceasefire remains on edge as both sides brace for potential escalation.

headlines.headline: Tech Rivalry Overshadows AI Push at Trump-Xi Summit
headlines.summary: US officials say AI will be central to Trump-Xi talks but substantive commitments are unlikely given deepening tech rivalry and mutual distrust. The strategic importance of artificial intelligence highlights the technology's role in great power competition.

perspectives.angle: Tech rivalry and distrust dim hopes for Trump-Xi AI cooperation
perspectives.summary: Trump will put AI at the forefront of talks with Xi, highlighting the technology's strategic heft, but substantive commitments are unlikely given deep mistrust and tech rivalry between the two powers.

headlines.headline: Bessent and Chinese VP hold talks in South Korea ahead of Trump-Xi summit
headlines.summary: US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng concluded three hours of talks in South Korea aimed at laying groundwork for this week's Beijing summit between Trump and Xi.

perspectives.angle: US and China trade chiefs hold constructive talks in Seoul before Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Vice-Premier He Lifeng met for three hours at Incheon Airport in what Chinese state media described as candid and constructive exchanges, setting groundwork for the Trump-Xi summit.

headlines.headline: Lukashenko Announces Military Mobilization as Belarus Prepares for War
headlines.summary: Belarusian President Alexander Lukashenko announced a rotational mobilization of selected military units, stating "We are all preparing for war" amid heightened regional tensions.

headlines.headline: Russian Nuclear Submarines Receive Anti-Drone Defenses Thousands of Miles From Ukraine
headlines.summary: New satellite imagery reveals anti-drone protections around Russian nuclear submarines, suggesting commanders fear Ukrainian drone capabilities extending far beyond the front lines.

headlines.headline: South Korea Weighs Phased Hormuz Support After US Talks
headlines.summary: South Korea is reviewing a phased contribution to efforts to ensure safe navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, signaling support steps short of military participation as the US seeks allies for the Iran mission.

perspectives.angle: South Korea Signals Phased Support for Hormuz Mission
perspectives.summary: South Korea announced phased support for the US-led Strait of Hormuz mission following a ship attack, joining international efforts to secure the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: Pentagon: US War in Iran Has Cost $29 Billion
headlines.summary: A senior Pentagon official said the US war in Iran has cost $29 billion so far, an increase of $4 billion from estimates late last month, as lawmakers query costs and Trump renews strike threats.

perspectives.angle: US Iran War Has Cost $29 Billion, Pentagon Tells Congress
perspectives.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified before Congress that the US military campaign against Iran has cost approximately $29 billion, while lawmakers questioned munitions readiness and escalation options.

headlines.headline: US and China Agree on Opposing Hormuz Shipping Tolls
headlines.summary: Senior US and Chinese officials agree that no country should exact shipping tolls in the Strait of Hormuz, a sign both countries are trying to find common ground on pressuring Iran to give up control of the vital waterway.

perspectives.angle: US and China agree no country should exact Hormuz shipping tolls
perspectives.summary: Senior US and Chinese officials agreed that no country should be allowed to impose shipping tolls in the Strait of Hormuz, signaling cooperation to pressure Iran over control of the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: UK Bond Vigilantes Exact Price on Government as Yields Hit 28-Year High
headlines.summary: Britain's long-dated government borrowing costs have surged to their highest since 1998 as concern over potential leadership change weakens fiscal discipline, with 30-year gilt yields hitting 5.81%.

perspectives.angle: UK Bond Yields Surge to 1998 High as Starmer's Leadership Faces Doubt
perspectives.summary: Long-term British borrowing costs hit their highest level in nearly 30 years while the pound slumped, as investors brace for potential leadership changes that could weaken fiscal discipline.

headlines.headline: G7 Long Bond Stress Intensifies Amid Rising Borrowing Costs
headlines.summary: The relentless rise in long-term government borrowing costs shows no sign of abating, with stress building in world markets as stock markets defy gravity.

headlines.headline: Hezbollah urges Lebanon to withdraw from direct Israel talks
headlines.summary: Hezbollah leader Naim Kassem called on Lebanon to withdraw from direct negotiations with Israel in Washington, advocating for indirect talks instead. The call comes amid Israeli strikes that have killed 380 people since the April 17 ceasefire.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon Enters Israel Talks With Little Room to Maneuver
perspectives.summary: Beirut heads into Washington negotiations constrained by hardening positions on both sides. Hezbollah has largely cut off contact with Lebanese authorities, while Israel shows little appetite for ending strikes soon as elections approach.

perspectives.angle: Lebanon and Israel Open New Talks as Ceasefire Nears End
perspectives.summary: Third round of US-mediated negotiations begins in Washington with over 400 killed during the ceasefire. Lebanon seeks ceasefire consolidation as Israel continues strikes against Hezbollah despite the truce.

headlines.headline: Putin hails successful test of Sarmat nuclear ICBM
headlines.summary: Russia successfully tested its new Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile, with President Putin calling it the most powerful in the world and announcing plans to deploy it by years end. The test comes as the last nuclear arms treaty with the US has expired.

perspectives.angle: Russia Successfully Tests Sarmat ICBM, Putin Claims Most Powerful Missile
perspectives.summary: Russia test-fired its new Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile, with President Vladimir Putin declaring it the worlds most powerful nuclear-capable missile. The test comes months after the last US-Russia nuclear arms treaty expired, raising concerns about an unconstrained nuclear arms race.

perspectives.angle: Russia plans to deploy Sarmat ICBM operationally later this year
perspectives.summary: Moscow announced plans for operational deployment of the Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile after claiming a successful test, despite years of technical issues and delays.

headlines.headline: Australia Deploys Surveillance Aircraft for Hormuz Mission
headlines.summary: Australia will deploy surveillance aircraft to support the international mission protecting the Strait of Hormuz amid ongoing Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Kuwait Arrests Four IRGC-Affiliated Individuals Attempting Sea Infiltration
headlines.summary: Kuwait's interior ministry announced the arrest of four people affiliated with Iran's Revolutionary Guards who attempted to infiltrate the Gulf state by sea, highlighting regional tensions and security concerns.

headlines.headline: Islamic State Claims Deadly Attack on Syrian Government Forces
headlines.summary: Islamic State claimed responsibility for an attack in eastern Syria that killed two Syrian army soldiers, the jihadist group's first deadly operation against the Syrian government since February.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia launched covert attacks on Iranian soil
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia carried out previously unreported military strikes inside Iran, marking the first time the kingdom is known to have directly attacked Iranian territory as the regional war expanded.

headlines.headline: Iran expands Strait of Hormuz operational zone definition
headlines.summary: A senior IRGC Navy officer says Iran has redefined the Strait of Hormuz as a "vast operational area" far wider than before the war, signaling potential escalation in the strategic waterway.

perspectives.angle: Iran redefines Strait of Hormuz boundaries 10 times wider
perspectives.summary: An IRGC officer says Iran now defines the Strait of Hormuz as 10 times wider than before the war, significantly expanding what Tehran considers its territorial waters. The move could escalate tensions with shipping in the critical waterway.

headlines.headline: US Defense Secretary Hegseth Joins Trump on Rare Beijing Trip
headlines.summary: Pete Hegseth becomes the first Pentagon chief to accompany a US president to Beijing since Nixon's 1972 visit, signaling the strategic importance of the Trump-Xi summit amid ongoing Iran tensions.

headlines.headline: Sirens Warn of Suspected Hezbollah Drone Attack in Northern Israel
headlines.summary: Air raid sirens sounded in northern Israel amid a suspected Hezbollah drone intrusion from Lebanon, raising fears of a new front opening as the Iran conflict continues.

headlines.headline: Ukrainian Drones Strike Port Infrastructure in Russia's Krasnodar Krai
headlines.summary: Ukrainian forces targeted port facilities near the Kerch Peninsula east of Crimea, continuing their campaign against Russian logistics in the occupied territories.

perspectives.angle: Ukrainian Drones Strike Russian Gas Facility 1,500km From Border
perspectives.summary: Ukrainian forces carried out drone strikes on a gas facility in Russia's Orenburg Oblast, more than 1,500 kilometers from the Ukrainian border, demonstrating continued deep-strike capabilities after the Victory Day ceasefire ended.

headlines.headline: Trump says Ukraine war very close to ending as Kremlin echoes claim
headlines.summary: President Donald Trump said the war in Ukraine is "very close" to ending, while the Kremlin repeated Putin's assertion that the conflict is nearly over. Ukrainian President Zelenskiy has expressed skepticism about Moscow's intentions.

perspectives.angle: Trump Says Russia-Ukraine War End Is Very Close
perspectives.summary: US President Donald Trump stated that he believes the Russia-Ukraine war is nearing an end and that Moscow and Kyiv are moving closer to a settlement, amid ongoing diplomatic efforts.

headlines.headline: US EIA warns Middle East oil disruptions worse than previously estimated
headlines.summary: The US Energy Information Administration revised forecasts to reflect a much larger and longer hit to global oil supplies from the Iran war than previously projected, with the Strait of Hormuz expected to remain shut through late May.

headlines.headline: Taiwan president thanks US for defense support ahead of Beijing summit
headlines.summary: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te expressed gratitude to the US for helping strengthen the island's defenses and said Taipei would not give in to pressure, ahead of the Trump-Xi summit in Beijing.

perspectives.angle: Taiwan civilians flock to self-defense courses ahead of Trump-Xi summit
perspectives.summary: Taiwanese civilians are enrolling in self-defense courses in record numbers amid fears China could use force to seize the island. The surge comes as Trump prepares to visit China for talks that could determine the future of US-Taiwan relations.

headlines.headline: Strait of Hormuz emerges as central battleground of Iran conflict
headlines.summary: Reuters analysis: The passage of a handful of oil and gas tankers in recent days with Tehran's consent hints at tacit acceptance of Iranian control, foreshadowing a more dangerous phase in what is fast becoming a Hormuz war.

headlines.headline: Iran sets five conditions for resuming talks with US
headlines.summary: Iran demands ending the war on all fronts, lifting sanctions, releasing frozen assets, compensating for war damage, and recognizing sovereignty rights over the Strait of Hormuz before any new negotiations with Washington.

headlines.headline: Ukraine sanctions 66 Russian entities over military supply chain
headlines.summary: President Zelensky imposed sanctions on 32 Russian companies and 34 individuals involved in supplying materials for ballistic missiles, surface-to-air systems, and drone production.

headlines.headline: EU expresses frustration over slow defense industry ramp-up
headlines.summary: EU diplomat Kaja Kallas said the bloc is frustrated that despite billions in funding, defense production lags behind Ukraine, which has become a significant military-industrial power in four years.

perspectives.angle: EU expresses frustration over failed defense industry ramp-up
perspectives.summary: EU top diplomat Kaja Kallas voiced frustration over the bloc's slow defense production pace, drawing contrast with Ukraine's rapid industrial growth amid the Russia war.

headlines.headline: Major fire breaks out at Moscow tourist complex
headlines.summary: A massive fire exceeding 3,000 square meters engulfed the Izmailovo Kremlin cultural and entertainment complex in eastern Moscow overnight, with the cause unclear.

headlines.headline: UK affirms Ukraine support amid domestic political turmoil
headlines.summary: UK Europe Minister Stephen Doughty assured continued British backing for Ukraine despite Labour Party losses in local elections and pressure on PM Starmer.

perspectives.angle: UK Minister Vows Continued Support for Ukraine Despite Domestic Political Turmoil
perspectives.summary: Europe Minister Stephen Doughty told the Kyiv Independent that Britain remains "resolutely behind Ukraine" even as Prime Minister Keir Starmer faces calls to resign after Labour losses in local elections.

headlines.headline: Trump Golden Dome missile shield to cost $1.2 trillion
headlines.summary: US Congressional Budget Office estimates Trump's proposed missile defense system will cost nearly seven times his initial estimate. The system may not be able to stop an all-out missile attack.

perspectives.angle: Trump Golden Dome missile shield could cost $1.2 trillion, watchdog warns
perspectives.summary: A US government watchdog estimates Trump's missile defense system will cost six times more than expected and could still be overwhelmed by a peer adversary's all-out attack, raising questions about feasibility.

headlines.headline: Global conflict displacements hit record 32.3 million in 2025
headlines.summary: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict reached a record high last year, surpassing disaster-driven displacement for the first time. Total global displacement now stands at 82.2 million.

perspectives.angle: Global conflict displacements hit record 32.3 million in 2025
perspectives.summary: Internal displacements from violence and conflict reached an all-time high in 2025, surpassing disaster-driven displacements for the first time. Iran and DR Congo accounted for two-thirds of all conflict-driven displacements.

headlines.headline: Uganda's Museveni Sworn In for Record Seventh Term After Disputed Election
headlines.summary: President Yoweri Museveni, 81, won January's disputed elections to extend his 40-year rule, making him one of Africa's longest-serving leaders.

perspectives.angle: Ugandas Museveni sworn in for record seventh term after disputed election
perspectives.summary: President Yoweri Museveni, 81, began his seventh consecutive term extending his 40-year rule over Uganda. Opposition leader Bobi Wine fled the country claiming the election was marred by ballot-stuffing, while rights groups reported at least 16 killed in post-election violence.

headlines.headline: Serbia Hosts First Joint Military Exercise With NATO Since Kosovo War
headlines.summary: The two-week drills mark a historic shift for Serbia, coming less than 30 years after NATO bombed Belgrade during the Kosovo conflict.

perspectives.angle: Serbia Hosts First Joint Military Exercise With NATO
perspectives.summary: Serbia conducted its first joint military drills with NATO, less than 30 years after the alliance bombed Belgrade during the Kosovo war, marking a significant shift in Belgrade's foreign policy alignment.

headlines.headline: Dali Ship Operator Charged Criminally for Baltimore Bridge Collapse
headlines.summary: Federal prosecutors filed criminal charges against Synergy Marine for the Key Bridge collapse that killed six workers. The company faces conspiracy, obstruction, and misconduct charges.

perspectives.angle: Dali ship operator charged over deadly Baltimore bridge collapse
perspectives.summary: Federal prosecutors filed criminal charges against Synergy Marine over the 2024 Key Bridge collapse that killed six workers. Maryland also reached a $2.25 billion civil settlement with the company.

headlines.headline: Bahamas Heads to Polls in Snap Election With Immigration as Key Issue
headlines.summary: Voters in the Bahamas are casting ballots in a three-way race dominated by concerns over immigration, the cost of living, and housing costs.

headlines.headline: NATO to Create Vetted Counter-Drone Vendor Pool for Member Nations
headlines.summary: NATO is establishing a common funding mechanism and approved vendor pool for counter-UAS systems, allowing member nations to test systems before procurement decisions.

perspectives.angle: NATO establishes counter-drone vendor pool for member nations
perspectives.summary: NATO has created a vetted vendor pool for counter-drone systems with common funding allowing nations to test C-UAS systems before procurement. The initiative aims to standardize allied air defenses.

headlines.headline: Turkey Announces Propellant Breakthrough for Yildirimhan Long-Range Ballistic Missile
headlines.summary: Turkish defense officials have announced a significant advancement in solid propellant technology for the Yildirimhan long-range ballistic missile program, boosting domestic missile development capabilities.

perspectives.angle: Turkey Unveils Yildirimhan ICBM With 6,000km Range
perspectives.summary: Turkey announced a major propellant breakthrough for its Yildirimhan intercontinental ballistic missile program, revealing capabilities previously undisclosed. The 6,000km range raises questions about Ankara's strategic ambitions.

headlines.headline: Russian ship that sank near Spain may have been carrying nuclear reactors to North Korea
headlines.summary: A Russian vessel that sank in the Mediterranean may have been transporting nuclear reactor components destined for North Korea, raising concerns about illicit nuclear proliferation and sanctions evasion.

perspectives.angle: Russian Ship That Sank Near Spain May Have Been Carrying Nuclear Reactors to North Korea
perspectives.summary: The cargo vessel Ursa Major, which sank off the Spanish coast, may have been transporting nuclear reactor components destined for North Korea, raising questions about Russia-North Korea military cooperation.

headlines.headline: French hantavirus patient on artificial lung as outbreak spreads to 11 cases
headlines.summary: A French woman infected with hantavirus on the MV Hondius cruise ship is in critical condition on an artificial lung, as the total number of confirmed cases grows to 11, prompting WHO to urge countries to prepare for more.

headlines.headline: 85-year-old French widow detained in Trump immigration crackdown
headlines.summary: An elderly French widow was caught up in the Trump immigration enforcement operations, describing her detention experience as the administration intensifies border enforcement.

headlines.headline: UK sends warship, jets and drones for Strait of Hormuz mission
headlines.summary: The UK pledged drones, fighter jets and a warship to a 40-nation mission to safeguard shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. HMS Dragon will join the defensive operation as Iran continues to control the vital waterway.

headlines.headline: US allies surpassed American military purchasing power in 2025, new report shows
headlines.summary: A new defense analysis reveals that US allies have collectively surpassed American military budget purchasing power, though Washington remains the single highest spender in absolute terms.

perspectives.angle: Allies surpass US in military budget purchasing power as NATO spending rises
perspectives.summary: A new report shows US allies have surpassed the US in combined military purchasing power, though Washington remains the single highest military spender globally.

headlines.headline: IRGC Navy claims vast expansion in its definition of Strait of Hormuz
headlines.summary: Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps navy has declared a tenfold increase in what it considers the Strait of Hormuz, raising questions about enforcement amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: EU Sanctions Hamas Leaders and Israeli Settler Organizations
headlines.summary: European Union foreign ministers unanimously agreed to sanction Hamas leaders and Israeli settler groups including Amana, Nachala, and Regavim over West Bank violence and Gaza devastation.

headlines.headline: Keir Starmer Faces Deep Political Peril After Surviving Resignation Calls
headlines.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer remains under intense pressure despite surviving calls for his resignation, as internal Labour divisions and policy controversies continue to mount.

headlines.headline: Israel Sent Iron Dome Batteries and Personnel to UAE
headlines.summary: US Ambassador Mike Huckabee revealed that Israel deployed Iron Dome anti-missile batteries and personnel to the UAE, calling on Gulf states to "pick a side" in the war between Israel and Iran.

perspectives.angle: Israel Deploys Iron Dome Systems and Personnel to UAE
perspectives.summary: Israel sent Iron Dome air-defense batteries and operators to the UAE during the Iran war, marking the first publicly acknowledged Israeli military deployment to the Emirates.

headlines.headline: Kevin Warsh Confirmed to Federal Reserve Board in Close Senate Vote
headlines.summary: The US Senate confirmed Kevin Warsh to the Federal Reserve Board in a narrow vote, with Democratic Senator John Fetterman joining Republicans to advance the nomination.

perspectives.angle: US Senate Confirms Kevin Warsh to Federal Reserve Board, Set to Replace Powell as Chair
perspectives.summary: The Senate confirmed Trump's pick Kevin Warsh to the Federal Reserve Board, clearing the way for him to take over as Fed chair from Jerome Powell later this week in a significant shift for US monetary policy.

headlines.headline: Saudi Arabia covertly struck Iran during US-Iran war, sources say
headlines.summary: Saudi Arabia conducted undisclosed strikes against Iran during the ongoing conflict, marking a shift from its traditional diplomatic stance as regional tensions escalate.

headlines.headline: US Weapons Stockpile Depletion Raises Concerns for Ukraine Aid
headlines.summary: Weeks of conflict with Iran have depleted critical US weapons stocks, leading European allies to question the viability of American support for Ukraine as the war stretches Pentagon supplies.

perspectives.angle: US Weapons Stockpiles Depleted by Iran War, Raising Concerns for Ukraine Support
perspectives.summary: As the Iran conflict stretches into its sixth week, US weapons stockpiles have been significantly depleted, raising concerns among European allies about continued military support for Ukraine. Key munitions including Patriot interceptors and artillery shells are running low.

headlines.headline: UK pledges jets, drones and warship for Hormuz defence mission
headlines.summary: The UK announced it will contribute Typhoon jets, mine-hunting drones and HMS Dragon destroyer to a 40-nation mission to secure the Strait of Hormuz. Defence Minister John Healey said the mission would begin when conditions allow, with £115m in new funding.

headlines.headline: Venezuelas acting president rejects Trumps 51st state comments
headlines.summary: Acting President Delcy Rodríguez told journalists at the International Court of Justice that Venezuela is "not a colony, but a free country" after Trump said he was "seriously considering" making Venezuela the 51st US state. She defended Venezuelas claim to the disputed Essequibo region in the territorial dispute with Guyana.

headlines.headline: US Finalizes $35.5 Billion in Refunds for Illegal Trump Tariffs
headlines.summary: US Customs and Border Protection has received 126,237 applications for tariff reimbursements as companies seek refunds for tariffs that were ruled illegal, a significant economic correction.

headlines.headline: Kuwait Foils Iranian Infiltration Attempt on Bubiyan Island
headlines.summary: Kuwait announced it arrested four men accused of being IRGC operatives attempting to infiltrate Bubiyan Island, marking a significant escalation as Gulf states risk being drawn into the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: UK PM Starmer Defies Calls to Resign After Heavy Election Losses
headlines.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to continue in office despite growing pressure from Labour Party lawmakers urging him to step down following devastating losses in local elections.

perspectives.angle: Keir Starmer Defies Calls to Resign After Labour Election Losses
perspectives.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer vowed to continue in office despite dozens of Labour Party lawmakers urging him to step down after heavy losses in local elections, in a significant political crisis for the UK government.

headlines.headline: WHO Warns Countries to Prepare for More Hantavirus Cases
headlines.summary: The World Health Organization urged nations to prepare for additional hantavirus cases as a cruise ship-linked outbreak spreads, with Spain confirming new cases bringing the total to 11.

headlines.headline: Hamas Weaponized Sexual Violence in October 7 Attacks, Israeli Investigation Finds
headlines.summary: A comprehensive Israeli report documents systematic sexual assaults during the October 7 attacks, detailing harrowing cases. It is the most extensive investigation of its kind to date.

headlines.headline: Sweden Detains Two for Helping Russia Evade Sanctions with High-Tech Shipments
headlines.summary: Swedish Security Service arrested two suspects, a Swedish and Turkish national, for violating EU sanctions by allegedly facilitating advanced technology shipments to Russia that could aid its war in Ukraine.

perspectives.angle: Sweden Detains Two Suspects Over Allegedly Aiding High-Tech Shipments to Russia
perspectives.summary: Swedish authorities have detained two people on suspicion of violating international sanctions by helping Russia procure advanced products, marking an escalation in enforcement of export controls.

headlines.headline: US Navy Reveals Trump Class Battleships Will Be Nuclear Powered
headlines.summary: The Navy annual shipbuilding plan confirms future Trump class BBGNs will use nuclear propulsion, reversing earlier position. Each 35,000-ton warship estimated at $17 billion with hypersonic missiles and railguns.

headlines.headline: IDF Uses Robots to Advance Beyond Lebanons Litani River
headlines.summary: Jerusalem Post reports Israeli forces are employing robotic systems to move north of the Litani River in Lebanon, with FPV drones continuing to challenge military operations in the area.

headlines.headline: Israel Killing One Palestinian Child Per Week in West Bank, UN Reports
headlines.summary: UN findings show Israeli military and settlers have killed at least one Palestinian child weekly on average in the occupied West Bank amid escalating violence.

headlines.headline: Turkey's Aselsan targets mass-producible networked weapons by 2027
headlines.summary: Turkish defense giant Aselsan aims to have new networked weapons systems in inventory by 2027, CEO Ahmet Akyol said. The company focuses on affordable, mass-producible solutions for export markets.

headlines.headline: US Navy signals openness to building ships overseas
headlines.summary: A new US Navy plan opens the door to building select ships overseas, marking a potential shift after reported disagreements between Trump and the previous Navy Secretary over outsourcing construction.

headlines.headline: Major Japanese oil firm expects Hormuz crisis to ease by July
headlines.summary: A major Japanese petroleum company forecasts the Strait of Hormuz disruption will subside by July, providing insight into Asian energy sector expectations amid the US-Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: Russia targets US firms in Ukraine as White House stays silent
headlines.summary: Russian forces have deliberately struck facilities tied to Coca-Cola, Cargill, Mondelez and other American companies in Ukraine. The Trump administration's muted response to these attacks on US corporate interests has raised concerns about its commitment to protecting American assets abroad.

headlines.headline: Supreme Court blocks Alabama congressional map with two Black districts
headlines.summary: The US Supreme Court halted a lower court order requiring Alabama to draw a congressional map with two majority-Black districts, a significant intervention in the ongoing redistricting battles ahead of the 2026 elections.

headlines.headline: Venezuela rejects Trump's 51st state remarks as territorial dispute escalates
headlines.summary: Venezuela's acting president firmly rejected President Trump's comments suggesting the country could become a US state, defending the nation's territorial integrity amid ongoing tensions over the Essequibo region bordering Guyana.

headlines.headline: WHO Warns Countries to Prepare for More Hantavirus Cases
headlines.summary: The WHO head said the situation could still change with more confirmed cases expected, as a cruise ship outbreak continues to raise concerns about the spread of the disease.

headlines.headline: IDF forces deploy 10km deep into Lebanese territory
headlines.summary: Israeli Defense Forces have crossed the Litani River and established positions 10 kilometers inside Lebanese territory, according to Haaretz. The deployment comes amid ongoing tensions with Hezbollah, though the militant group says disarmament is not part of Israel-Lebanon talks.

perspectives.angle: IDF Forces Deploy 10 Km Deep Into Lebanese Territory After Crossing Litani River
perspectives.summary: Israeli forces have advanced deep into southern Lebanon, crossing the Litani River and establishing positions 10 kilometers inside Lebanese territory amid ongoing tensions with Hezbollah.

headlines.headline: Ukraine drones hit Russian city 1,200km from front lines
headlines.summary: Russian cities Orenburg and Chelyabinsk, located over 1,200 kilometers from Ukraine, were reportedly struck by drones after the Victory Day ceasefire ended. The attacks demonstrate Ukraine's expanding long-range strike capabilities deep into Russian territory.

headlines.headline: Spain reports new hantavirus case as outbreak grows to 11
headlines.summary: Spain's hantavirus outbreak has grown to 11 confirmed cases, with the WHO chief saying there is currently no sign of a larger outbreak. Health authorities are monitoring the situation closely as the rare viral disease spreads.

headlines.headline: EU Rejects Putin's Proposal for Ex-German Leader Schroeder in Future Security Talks
headlines.summary: EU ministers dismissed Russia's suggestion that former German Chancellor Gerhard Schroeder play a role in future security discussions, citing his close relationship with Putin.

headlines.headline: UK Prime Minister Starmer Refuses to Resign Amid Growing Leadership Crisis
headlines.summary: British PM Keir Starmer faces mounting pressure to step down but says he will not resign, in what media describe as the most serious political crisis of his tenure.

perspectives.angle: UK Prime Minister Starmer Defies Calls to Resign After Election Losses
perspectives.summary: Keir Starmer vowed to continue as prime minister despite dozens of Labour Party lawmakers urging him to step down following heavy local election defeats.

headlines.headline: Google Thwarts Zero-Day Attack Developed With AI
headlines.summary: Google says it stopped a sophisticated zero-day hack that was likely developed with AI assistance, marking one of the first confirmed cases of AI-assisted cyberattacks in the wild.

headlines.headline: Canvas Learning Platform Reaches Deal With Hackers to Prevent Massive Data Leak
headlines.summary: Instructure, which owns the Canvas learning platform used by thousands of schools, paid hackers to prevent the release of 3.65TB of student data after a massive breach.

headlines.headline: Texas Sues Netflix for Spying on Users Including Children
headlines.summary: Texas filed a lawsuit against Netflix alleging the streaming giant illegally collected data on users including children, amid growing scrutiny of platform surveillance practices.

headlines.headline: Former World Bank Chief Says China Should Stop Hoarding Food and Fertilizer
headlines.summary: David Malpass criticized China strategic stockpiling of food and fertilizer, saying its claim to developing nation status was no longer credible.

headlines.headline: California Mayor Resigns After Pleading Guilty to Acting as Chinese Agent
headlines.summary: Eileen Wang, mayor of Arcadia, California, agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for the Chinese government. She was accused of promoting pro-Beijing propaganda without notifying US authorities, including content denying Uyghur genocide.

perspectives.angle: California Mayor Resigns, Will Plead Guilty to Acting as Chinese Agent
perspectives.summary: Eileen Wang, mayor of Arcadia, California, resigned and agreed to plead guilty to charges of acting as an illegal agent of the Chinese government while serving in public office.

headlines.headline: France Announces $27 Billion Investment Package at Africa Summit in Kenya
headlines.summary: President Macron co-hosted the Africa Forward Summit in English-speaking Kenya for the first time, marking a shift away from traditional Francophone Africa ties. The move comes amid rising anti-French sentiment in former colonies.

headlines.headline: US in Secret Talks to Open Three New Military Bases in Greenland
headlines.summary: The US is negotiating with Denmark to establish three new military bases in southern Greenland, focused on surveillance of Russian and Chinese activity in the GIUK Gap. The talks aim to resolve tensions after Trump threatened to seize the territory.

perspectives.angle: US in Talks With Denmark to Open Three New Military Bases in Greenland
perspectives.summary: The US is negotiating with Denmark to establish three new military bases in southern Greenland, focusing on surveillance of Russian and Chinese activity in the Arctic. The talks aim to resolve diplomatic tensions after Trump's threat to seize the territory.

headlines.headline: Bolivia Issues Arrest Warrant for Former President Evo Morales
headlines.summary: A Bolivian judge reissued an arrest warrant for ex-President Evo Morales after he failed to appear for his trial on charges of trafficking a minor. Morales is accused of fathering a child with a 15-year-old girl while in office.

headlines.headline: South Korea Cautious on Blaming Iran for Ship Strike Ahead of US-China Summit
headlines.summary: South Korea has footage of the May 4 attack on the HMM Namu vessel but analysts say Seoul is biding its time, hoping the situation will settle as Trump and Xi prepare to meet in Beijing.

headlines.headline: Unitree Debuts $574K Transforming Mecha Robot in China
headlines.summary: Chinese robotics company Unitree has unveiled a $574,000 GD01 mecha robot that transforms from two legs to four, expanding its portfolio as Chinese manufacturers significantly outpace US rivals in robotics.

headlines.headline: China Deepens AI Footprint at Conference Despite US Tensions
headlines.summary: Alibaba, ByteDance and other Chinese tech giants topped paper submissions at the NeurIPS conference, quelling concerns that geopolitical tensions would impact participation in global AI research.

headlines.headline: European Airlines Adding China Flights Despite Russian Airspace Barrier
headlines.summary: Demand for China-Europe flights is surging as airlines avoid flying over Iranian airspace and divert tourists away from Southeast Asia, with carriers expanding routes despite Russian airspace restrictions.

headlines.headline: EU imposes sanctions on Israeli settlers over West Bank violence
headlines.summary: The European Union agreed to sanction Israeli settlers accused of violence against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank. Israel called the move "arbitrary" while the EU foreign policy chief said "extremism and violence carry consequences."

perspectives.angle: EU Imposes Sanctions on Israeli Settlers and Hamas Members
perspectives.summary: The European Union approved sanctions targeting Israeli settlers and organizations in the West Bank as well as Hamas members, after Hungary dropped its veto that had blocked the measures.

headlines.headline: Pakistan airstrike killed 269 Afghans at rehab centre, families demand answers
headlines.summary: A March 16 Pakistani airstrike on what officials called a militant target killed 269 Afghans at a rehabilitation centre. The UN says the death toll may be higher, with calls mounting to investigate the strike as a war crime.

headlines.headline: Duterte drug war enforcer escapes ICC arrest in Philippines
headlines.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, former national police chief under ex-President Duterte, has sought refuge in the Philippine senate to avoid arrest by the International Criminal Court over his role in the deadly drug war.

perspectives.angle: Philippine senator flees ICC arrest over Duterte drug war role
perspectives.summary: Senator Ronald Dela Rosa has evaded a warrant related to the drug war during Duterte presidency, fleeing abroad as ICC intensifies its investigation.

headlines.headline: US says Pakistan allowed Iran to park military aircraft on its airbases
headlines.summary: A US report claims Pakistan permitted Iran to station military aircraft on Pakistani territory during the conflict. The allegation could strain US-Pakistan relations and raises questions about regional alignments during the Iran crisis.

headlines.headline: France moves aircraft carrier to Red Sea amid Hormuz crisis
headlines.summary: France has deployed its aircraft carrier to the Red Sea, positioning it for potential operations near the Strait of Hormuz as the US-Iran conflict continues. The move underscores European concerns about energy security and freedom of navigation.

headlines.headline: Asia Braces for Second Wave of Energy Shocks from Iran War
headlines.summary: Countries across Asia are preparing for another round of energy disruptions as the Iran conflict continues to threaten oil and gas supplies through critical waterways.

perspectives.angle: UK Energy Secretary vows permanent North Sea shutdown amid Iran war supply crunch
perspectives.summary: Ed Miliband pledged to permanently shut down North Sea oil and gas production, though timing draws controversy as the Iran war disrupts Middle East energy supplies.

perspectives.angle: OPEC Oil Output at 26-Year Low Amid Iran War, Reuters Survey Shows
perspectives.summary: OPEC oil production has fallen to its lowest level in 26 years as the Iran conflict disrupts regional supply chains and the Strait of Hormuz blockade continues to impact global energy markets.

headlines.headline: Israel Passes Death Penalty Law for October 7-Linked Defendants
headlines.summary: Israel Knesset passes law permitting death penalty and public trials for those involved in the Hamas-led October 7 attacks, a major shift in Israeli judicial policy.

perspectives.angle: Israeli parliament approves special tribunal with death penalty for October 7 attackers
perspectives.summary: Israeli lawmakers backed the creation of a special tribunal with authority to impose death sentences on those accused of involvement in the October 7 attacks, a controversial move that human rights groups have criticized.

headlines.headline: Duterte Drug War Enforcer Evades ICC Arrest in Philippines
headlines.summary: Senator Ronald "Bato" Dela Rosa, former national police chief under Duterte, sought refuge in the Philippine senate to avoid arrest warrant from the International Criminal Court.

headlines.headline: Philippine VP Sara Duterte Impeached for Second Time
headlines.summary: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a second impeachment complaint, which if convicted in a Senate trial would disqualify her from running for president in future elections.

perspectives.angle: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte impeached for second time
perspectives.summary: The Philippine House of Representatives impeached Vice President Sara Duterte for a second time on charges of misusing public funds and threatening the lives of President Marcos Jr and his wife. If convicted in a Senate trial, she would be disqualified from running for president.

headlines.headline: Canada Seeks Defense Partnership With Turkey as Middle Powers Align
headlines.summary: Canada is rapidly expanding defense ties with Turkey, viewing Ankara as a trusted middle-power partner for co-development of drones, counter-drone systems, and ammunition amid shifting global security dynamics and US retrenchment.

perspectives.angle: Canada Eyes Turkey as Defense Industry Partner, Official Says
