perspectives.summary: Canada is seeking lessons from Turkey's homegrown defense industrial ecosystem, with a senior Canadian official saying Ottawa looks to Ankara as a model for developing vital defense capabilities as a mid-power ally.

headlines.headline: Russia Paid North Korea Up to $13.8 Billion for War Support, South Korean Intel Says
headlines.summary: North Korea has earned between $7-13.8 billion supplying weapons including artillery and ballistic missiles to Russia, with an estimated 6,000 North Korean casualties in Ukraine and plans to send 30,000 more soldiers.

perspectives.angle: Russia paid North Korea up to $13.8B for war support
perspectives.summary: North Korea has earned billions for supplying weapons and soldiers to Russia's war in Ukraine, according to a new South Korean intelligence report.

headlines.headline: UK Sanctions 85 People and Entities Over Russian Information Warfare
headlines.summary: Britain imposed new sanctions targeting Russian propaganda operations including attempts to interfere in Armenian elections, alongside measures against those involved in the forced deportation and indoctrination of Ukrainian children.

perspectives.angle: UK sanctions 85 people and entities over pro-Kremlin narratives
perspectives.summary: British government targets individuals and organizations accused of helping Russia forcibly deport and militarize Ukrainian children, calling it one of its "toughest measures" to date.

headlines.headline: South Africa's Ramaphosa Refuses to Resign Despite Impeachment Push
headlines.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he will legally challenge a report paving the way for impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala farm scandal. He vowed to fight the findings which alleged serious misconduct.

perspectives.angle: South Africa's Ramaphosa Defies Calls to Resign Over Cash-in-Sofa Scandal
perspectives.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa refused to resign after a constitutional court ruling revived impeachment proceedings over the Farmgate scandal, in which thieves stole foreign currency hidden in a sofa at his ranch.

perspectives.angle: South Africa President Ramaphosa Vows to Fight Impeachment, Won't Resign
perspectives.summary: President Cyril Ramaphosa said he will legally challenge a report paving the way for impeachment proceedings over the Phala Phala cash theft scandal. The Constitutional Court ruled parliament must consider impeachment, but Ramaphosa declared "I remain here and am not resigning."

headlines.headline: Trump-Xi Summit Could Define Superpower Relations for Years, BBC Analysis Says
headlines.summary: The first US presidential visit to China in nearly 10 years comes as Trump focuses on Iran, military operations, and domestic concerns. Taiwan, trade tensions, and technology competition are at stake, with both leaders seeking cooperation on the Iran conflict.

headlines.headline: China Passenger Car Exports Surge 85% in April as Domestic Sales Slump
headlines.summary: China exported 796,000 vehicles in April, up 85% year-on-year, with EV exports jumping 120%. Domestic sales fell 25.5% amid reduced subsidies and economic uncertainty. Analysts expect continued export growth despite trade tensions.

headlines.headline: Trump Announces Plan to Suspend Federal Gas Tax Amid Soaring Fuel Prices
headlines.summary: President Trump said he would suspend the 18-cent federal gasoline tax to offset prices that have risen 50% since the Iran war began. The move requires congressional approval, with Senator Hawley planning legislation. Gas prices now average $4.52 per gallon.

headlines.headline: Trump Nominates Kari Lake as US Ambassador to Jamaica
headlines.summary: Former Arizona news anchor and Trump ally Kari Lake has been nominated as ambassador to Jamaica. Lake recently led efforts to dismantle Voice of America, terminating hundreds of employees. She thanked Trump, saying Jamaica "is a country I know very well."

headlines.headline: Nearly 200 Sanctioned Russian Ships Entered UK Waters Despite Warning
headlines.summary: BBC Verify analysis shows 184 UK-sanctioned "shadow fleet" vessels made 238 journeys through UK waters since March, with no public evidence of boardings. Critics call lack of action "pathetic" amid legal constraints.

headlines.headline: California Mayor Resigns, Pleads Guilty to Acting as Chinese Agent
headlines.summary: Arcadia Mayor Eileen Wang agreed to plead guilty to acting as an illegal agent for China, resigning from her position. She was accused of promoting pro-PRC propaganda through a news website targeting Chinese Americans, including content denying Uyghur persecution.

headlines.headline: Ukraine turns to Europe for possible airport ceasefire with Russia
headlines.summary: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha told Politico that Europe may help mediate an agreement between Ukraine and Russia to halt attacks on each other's airports.

perspectives.angle: Three-Day Ceasefire Ends as Russia Launches Fresh Drone Strikes on Ukraine
perspectives.summary: At least one person was killed as Moscow launched drone strikes on energy facilities and apartments following the end of a brief ceasefire, renewing attacks on Ukrainian infrastructure.

headlines.headline: Hungary vows to stop abusing EU veto rights, incoming foreign minister says
headlines.summary: Hungary's incoming foreign minister Anita Orban stated that Hungary has too often used its veto "as political theater" and pledged to change course during a confirmation hearing.

perspectives.angle: Hungary pledges to stop abusing EU veto rights
perspectives.summary: Hungary incoming foreign minister Anita Orban told parliament that Hungary has too often used the veto as political theater, signaling a shift in EU relations under the new Magyar government.

headlines.headline: Epic Fury deployment has US Navy rethinking carrier deployment tempo
headlines.summary: The record-breaking deployment of the USS Gerald R. Ford has reopened debate about how the Navy should structure deployments and time at home for carrier strike groups.

headlines.headline: Morgan Stanley warns oil buffers could run out before Hormuz reopens
headlines.summary: Most market buffers preventing oil futures from hitting record highs could vanish before the Strait of Hormuz reopens, creating potential for severe price spikes.

headlines.headline: UK sanctions Iran-linked network for planning attacks
headlines.summary: Britain froze assets and imposed travel bans on individuals accused of laundering billions and plotting Iranian-backed hostile activity on UK soil.

headlines.headline: Musk, Cook among 17 CEOs to join Trump on China visit
headlines.summary: Top US executives including Elon Musk (Tesla/SpaceX), Tim Cook (Apple), and Larry Fink (BlackRock) will accompany President Trump to Beijing. The trip comes amid fragile tariff truce and Iranian war tensions affecting US-China relations.

perspectives.angle: Trump visits Beijing with Musk, Cook and top CEOs for Xi summit
perspectives.summary: President Trump arrives in China for his first visit since returning to office, accompanied by Elon Musk, Tim Cook and other major business leaders. The summit will address trade, Taiwan, and the future of US-China relations amid ongoing tensions.

headlines.headline: White House press dinner shooting suspect pleads not guilty
headlines.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, pleaded not guilty to federal gun charges and attempted assassination of President Trump after allegedly firing at a Secret Service agent during the White House Correspondents' dinner.

perspectives.angle: Washington Dinner Shooting Suspect Pleads Not Guilty to Assassination Attempt on Trump
perspectives.summary: Cole Tomas Allen, 31, entered a not guilty plea after being charged with attempting to assassinate President Donald Trump during a Washington dinner event.

headlines.headline: Dozens of Nigerian Fishermen Feared Dead After Chad Air Strikes on Boko Haram
headlines.summary: A fishermen's leader reports that 40 people are feared dead after Chad conducted air strikes on Boko Haram positions near Lake Chad, either from the strikes or drowning while fleeing.

perspectives.angle: Chad airstrikes on Boko Haram kill dozens of Nigerian fishermen
perspectives.summary: Fishermen's leader reports 40 people feared dead after Chad targeted Boko Haram militants near Lake Chad, with casualties including Nigerian civilians caught in strikes or drowning while fleeing.

headlines.headline: Mossad Chief Warns Against Gofman Appointment in Letter to High Court
headlines.summary: Outgoing Mossad chief sent a letter to Israel's High Court warning of "very great risk" from the appointment of Gofman, according to Haaretz. The warning comes amid significant security leadership transitions in Israel.
